description: Changed warmdown_iters from 500 to 700 to test whether a longer learning rate warmdown improves post-quantization val_bpb; the validated frontier used 600, so this probes the next point in the sweep (priority #1 in lane guidance).

Enabled gradient clipping with grad_clip_norm=0.3 (was 0.0/disabled); competitive PRs highlight this value as part of strong record-track recipes, and it's a zero-throughput-cost, zero-size-risk optimizer stabilization change.

Increased muon_momentum from 0.95 to 0.99; competitive record-track PRs highlight this value as part of strong recipes, and higher momentum in the Muon optimizer accumulates more gradient history for the Newton-Schulz orthogonalization, potentially improving convergence at zero throughput cost.

Changed warmdown_iters from 700 to 600 (the validated frontier value); experiment #1 tested 700 before grad_clip_norm=0.3 and muon_momentum=0.99 were added, so this tests whether the frontier-optimal warmdown length performs better with the current improved optimizer settings.

Changed warmdown_iters from 600 to 550 to test whether a shorter warmdown period (more steps at full learning rate) improves convergence on the short 1xH100 proxy; this is the next untested point in the warmdown sweep around 600, which is priority #1 in the lane guidance.

Changed warmdown_iters from 600 to 550; the warmdown sweep around 600 is the #1 lane priority and we've tested 700 and 600 but not values below 600 — this probes whether a slightly shorter warmdown yields better convergence with the current optimizer settings (grad_clip_norm=0.3, muon_momentum=0.99).

Changed matrix_lr from 0.08 to 0.075 to begin a narrow learning-rate sweep around the current value; this is priority #2 in the lane guidance after the warmdown sweep converged at 600, and is a zero-throughput-cost, zero-size-risk optimizer tuning change.

Increased matrix_lr from 0.08 to 0.085; this is the next priority (narrow matrix_lr sweep around 0.08) per lane guidance, and with grad_clip_norm=0.3 and muon_momentum=0.99 already providing training stability, a slightly higher Muon learning rate may allow faster convergence within the short 1xH100 proxy horizon at zero throughput cost.

Changed matrix_lr from 0.08 to 0.085 to sweep the Muon learning rate in the upward direction; 0.075 tied at 1.4967 without improvement, so this tests whether a slightly higher learning rate improves early convergence under the current optimizer settings (grad_clip_norm=0.3, muon_momentum=0.99).

Reduced muon_momentum_warmup_steps from 500 to 300 so that the Muon optimizer reaches its target momentum (0.99) within the ~300-step 1xH100 proxy window; at 500 warmup steps the momentum only reached ~0.934 by step 300, leaving significant optimizer capacity untapped during the proxy run. This is a zero-throughput-cost, zero-size-risk schedule change.

Increased muon_momentum_warmup_start from 0.85 to 0.90 so the Muon optimizer begins with higher momentum from step 0; experiments #3 and #10 showed that higher momentum and faster warmup both improve convergence, so starting the linear ramp from a higher baseline should further reduce the low-momentum "cold start" penalty during the short ~300-step proxy window, at zero throughput and size cost.

Raised muon_momentum_warmup_start from 0.85 to 0.90; with warmup_steps=300 matching the proxy window, a higher starting momentum means the optimizer accumulates more gradient history from the very first step, which should improve early convergence at zero throughput and zero size cost.

Increased tied_embed_lr from 0.05 to 0.06 to test whether a higher embedding learning rate improves convergence in the short ~300-step proxy window; with tied embeddings serving as both input and output projection, the embedding table is a critical parameter that has been completely unexplored across all 11 prior experiments, making this a zero-throughput-cost, zero-size-risk optimizer tuning change.

Increased tied_embed_lr from 0.06 to 0.07 to continue the embedding learning rate sweep; experiment #13 showed a clear improvement (1.4951→1.4937) when raising this from 0.05 to 0.06, so this tests whether the trend continues at the next point in the sweep, at zero throughput and zero size cost.

Increased tied_embed_lr from 0.06 to 0.07 to continue the embedding learning rate sweep; experiment #13 showed 0.06 improved over the default 0.05 (1.4937 vs 1.4951), so this tests whether pushing the embedding learning rate higher further improves convergence in the short ~300-step proxy window, at zero throughput and zero size cost.
